subject:
The worse average after Warp extracted subtomo average compared the
 dynamo average
body:
Hello
I am using Warp_Dynamo_Relion workflow using #teamtomo workflow for 
Cryo-FIB-ET data set. 
Here is the comparison of the (A)Warp-extracted-Relion_reconstructed 
average (shown yellow) and (B) Dynamo extracted average (shown blue). Both 
averages are just after cropping without additional alignment. In A, the 
overall alignment (like arch shape) is not completely lost but clearly 
loose the obvious features seen in B. 

Is this expected behavior or do I have some procedure error /data problem 
during processing?

 I obtained these averages as following.
1) Warp preprocessing (version 1.09) of frames, creation of tilt series.
2) Perform alignment with etomo
3) import alignment to Warp and reconstruct 4 bin tomo using Warp tomogram 
reconstruct.
4) Using this 4 bin tomogram, I performed Dynamo average. 
5) Using the refined dynamo table(4bin), I proceeded two ways. 
*A)* Convert 4 bin Dynamo table to Warp compatible star file (Dynamo2warp) 
and extract *subtomogram* as 2 bin using Warp. Perform Relion_reconstruct 
with (--ctf, --3d_rot option: note with or without these options did not 
change outcome the data). 
*B) *Convert 4bin Dynamo refined table to 2 bin compatible table by 
multiplying coordinates and shifts by 2. Reconstruct *2 bin tomogram* using 
the same Warp process file(same tomostar file used in A). Extract 
subtomogram using Dynamo crop function and pick crop.em average found in 
the cropped particles folder. Read crop em, put the  2 bin pix size in 
ChimeraX and export as mrc.

Since original averages from A and B are rather noisy (I used rather small 
data set), for visualization purpose, I did perform low pass filter using 
relion_image_handler. The averages shown here are 15A lowpass filtered.

I had similar issue with Relion4 (after psuedo subtomo extraction, 
reconstructed average looks lost feature although overall placement like 
arch shape seems OK). But in this case, it is more difficult to perform 
direct comparison since I also convert tomogram star file compatible with 
Relion4. 

I understand that Warp tomogram is corrected with 2DCTF and Warp 
subtomogram is 3DCTF corrected. Am I right? This could cause the difference?

Any suggestions are helpful! Thank you very much!

Reika
